description:
You are a platform engineer doing a quick clean-up of a CSV that drives several CI/CD dashboards.  
A source file already exists at  

    /home/user/ci_cd/build_stats.csv  

Your goal is to create a trimmed version of that file that contains only three columns, in the same order in which they appear in the source file:

1. BuildID  
2. Status  
3. DurationSec  

Save the result (including the header row) to  

    /home/user/ci_cd/build_stats_compact.csv  

Output format that the automated checker will look for:

• The output file must be plain text, UTF-8 encoded.  
• Columns are separated with a literal comma “,” and **no spaces**.  
• Each record (including the header) ends with a single Unix newline “\n”; no extra blank lines at the end.  
• The header row must be exactly:  

  BuildID,Status,DurationSec  

• The four data rows must appear in the same order as in the source file.

Example of the complete expected file:

BuildID,Status,DurationSec  
101,success,245  
102,failed,130  
103,success,300  
104,success,210

Use any standard command-line tool(s) you like, but this can be done with a single pipeline that relies on “cut”.  
When you are done, verify that “/home/user/ci_cd/build_stats_compact.csv” exists and that its contents match the format shown above.
truth:

File:

Exact contents of /home/user/ci_cd/build_stats.csv:
BuildID,Branch,CommitHash,Author,Status,DurationSec,ArtifactsSizeMB,Timestamp
101,main,ab12cd3,alice,success,245,120,2023-08-23T10:15:31Z
102,develop,bc34de5,bob,failed,130,0,2023-08-23T10:20:12Z
103,main,cd56ef7,charlie,success,300,98,2023-08-23T10:50:45Z
104,hotfix,ef78gh9,dana,success,210,110,2023-08-23T11:15:02Z

Required end state after the agent completes:

A new file must exist:
/home/user/ci_cd/build_stats_compact.csv  (permissions 0644, UTF-8)

Exact required contents of /home/user/ci_cd/build_stats_compact.csv:
BuildID,Status,DurationSec
101,success,245
102,failed,130
103,success,300
104,success,210

No additional leading/trailing whitespace or blank lines are allowed.
